title: Termination Reference to Andhra Pradesh Standard Specifications (APSS) Clauses 60 & 61
text_english: SUSPENSION of CONTRACTOR's RIGHTS: Where (i) the Contractor SUSPENDS the works WITHOUT SANCTION; (ii) SUB-LETS the work without sanction of the Engineer-in-Charge; (iii) NEGLECTS or FAILS to PROCEED with DUE DILIGENCE; (iv) CONTINUES to DEFAULT or REPEATS DEFAULTS per CLAUSE 26 of APSS — the ENGINEER-IN-CHARGE SHALL TAKE ACTION per CLAUSE 61 of APSS. (a) If Contractor STOPS WORK for {{stoppage_days}} (default 28) DAYS without Engineer's authorisation, the Contract SHALL be TERMINATED under CLAUSES 60 & 61 of APSS. (b) If Contractor has DELAYED COMPLETION, the Contract SHALL be TERMINATED under Clauses 60 & 61 of APSS. (c) Where Contractor causes a FUNDAMENTAL BREACH, the Department/Authority MAY TERMINATE the Contract; for CONVENIENCE, the Authority may terminate at ANY TIME without prior notice. APSS reference document: {{apss_version}}.

parameters.name: apss_version
parameters.label: APSS Version Referenced
parameters.example: Andhra Pradesh Standard Specifications, R&B Department, latest amendment
